summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV sightings across several front‑line oblasts and attacks on Russian‑linked fuel depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk. President Zelensky said Russia is reallocating hundreds of S‑400/S‑500 air‑defence missiles from other regions to protect Moscow and Valdai. Humanitarian workers were killed in an attack on a de‑mining mission in Kherson.
key_points: Ukrainian forces reported UAVs flying north of Tomakivka in Dnipropetrovsk and near Bogodukhiv in Kharkiv, heading south (Source: @kpszsu/66324).
Russian air‑defence assets, including S‑400 and S‑500 systems, are being moved from other Russian regions to protect Moscow and Valdai (Source: @UkraineNow/82154).
Russian‑linked fuel depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk were attacked by drones (Source: @UkraineNow/82159).
Two Norwegian People’s Aid staff were killed and four wounded in Kherson during a de‑mining operation (Source: @UkraineNow/82166).
Ukrainian intelligence noted difficulties implementing mandatory 60‑day rotation for front‑line troops (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31165).
A reactive Shahed missile was reported over Poltava (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68033).
France detained the tanker Deliver near Sicily, alleged to belong to a Russian shadow fleet (Source: @tass_agency/382412).
Ukrainian officials warned that renewed large‑scale strikes on the energy sector could cause power outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31162).

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions across Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, Sumy and Zaporizhzhia, indicating heightened aerial activity on the front. President Zelensky disclosed that Russia is concentrating hundreds of S‑400/S‑500 air‑defence missiles around Moscow and Valdai, reducing coverage elsewhere. Humanitarian de‑mining teams suffered casualties in Kherson, underscoring the growing risk to non‑combatants.

points: Ukrainian troops observed a UAV passing Tomakivka in Dnipropetrovsk, heading north (Source: @kpszsu/66324).
In Kharkiv's Bogodukhiv district, a UAV was seen moving southward (Source: @kpszsu/66302).
Sumy region reported a UAV near Krivyi Rih moving toward Putyvl and Bilopillia (Source: @eRadarrua/98087).
A reactive Shahed missile was detected over Poltava, prompting air‑defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68033).
Ukrainian officials warned that renewed massive strikes on the energy sector could trigger widespread power outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31162).
Ukrainian intelligence noted challenges in enforcing the 60‑day rotation order for front‑line units (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31165).
Ukrainian de‑mining teams in Kherson were attacked, killing two and wounding four humanitarian workers (Source: @UkraineNow/82166).
Russia is reallocating hundreds of S‑400 and S‑500 air‑defence missiles from other regions to protect Moscow and Valdai (Source: @UkraineNow/82154).
French authorities detained the tanker Deliver near Sicily, alleging it belongs to a Russian shadow fleet (Source: @tass_agency/382412).
Russian officials reported no Russian casualties from the Venezuelan earthquake (Source: @tass_agency/382409).
Russia plans to close the Romanian consulate in St. Petersburg (Source: @tass_agency/382400).
Apple removed Russian apps from the App Store, prompting Kremlin criticism (Source: @UkraineNow/82173).
Russian Ministry of Digital Development called the Apple removals politically motivated (Source: @UkraineNow/82173).
Russia announced a tax deduction for pet shelter owners (Source: @tass_agency/382410).
UAV activity reported near Tomakivka (Dnipropetrovsk) heading north (Source: @kpszsu/66324).

A UAV was sighted near Zaporizhzhia heading toward Balabyne (Source: @kpszsu/66334).
Reactive Shahed missile over Poltava indicates missile threat from the east (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68033).
Ukrainian forces reported a UAV approaching Malokaterynivka in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @eRadarrua/98117).
Ukrainian intelligence highlighted rotation enforcement issues for troops on the eastern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31165).
UAV detected on the north of Chernihiv heading west (Source: @kpszsu/66338).

UAV observed near Poltava heading north (Source: @eRadarrua/98110).

Ukrainian officials warned of potential large‑scale energy sector strikes causing outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31162).
Reactive Shahed missile over Poltava indicates continued missile threats (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68033).
Ukrainian UAV sightings across multiple fronts suggest increased reconnaissance activity (Source: multiple kpszsu posts).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces reported UAVs over Tomakivka (Dnipropetrovsk) heading north, indicating increased aerial reconnaissance (Source: @kpszsu/66324).
Russia is concentrating hundreds of S‑400 and S‑500 missiles around Moscow and Valdai, pulling them from other fronts (Source: @UkraineNow/82154).
Drones struck fuel depots in Zaporizhzhia, Mykolaiv and Dnipropetrovsk, targeting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82159).
Two Norwegian People’s Aid staff were killed in Kherson during a de‑mining mission, highlighting humanitarian risk (Source: @UkraineNow/82166).
A reactive Shahed missile was detected over Poltava, prompting air‑defence alerts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68033).
France detained the tanker Deliver near Sicily, alleging it belongs to a Russian shadow fleet (Source: @tass_agency/382412).
Ukrainian officials warned that renewed massive strikes on the energy sector could cause widespread power outages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31162).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian UAV activity across multiple oblasts suggests intensified ISR and potential strike preparation, while Russian air‑defence redeployment to protect Moscow creates gaps on other fronts. Drone attacks on fuel depots demonstrate Ukraine's capability to disrupt Russian logistics. The presence of reactive Shahed missiles and reported Russian tactical aviation activity indicates a continued missile threat to rear areas.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is reallocating air‑defence assets to cover gaps left by Russian missile shifts and increasing drone strikes on logistical targets. International partners, notably the EU, have provided €3.2 billion in aid, including funding for drone production. Humanitarian organizations face heightened risk, as evidenced by casualties in Kherson.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths should focus on north‑south corridors in Dnipropetrovsk and east‑west routes in Sumy for potential strike vectors. Russian S‑400/S‑500 concentration around Moscow may be vulnerable to long‑range missile attacks. The detention of the Deliver tanker suggests increased French naval vigilance against Russian shadow fleet movements.
